concepts.name: AI Triad
concepts.description: The three interacting components of Data, Algorithm, and Machine that define every ML system's physical constraints.

concepts.name: ML Lifecycle
concepts.description: A structured, iterative six-stage process guiding the development, evaluation, and improvement of machine learning systems.

concepts.name: ML Workflow
concepts.description: The engineering discipline of orchestrating the ML lifecycle by making constraints explicit and managing them across stages.

concepts.name: Stage Interface Contracts
concepts.description: Explicit input requirements, output deliverables, and quality invariants that must hold before advancing to the next lifecycle stage.

concepts.name: Constraint Propagation
concepts.description: The principle that late-stage constraint discoveries create exponentially escalating costs by forcing revisions across preceding stages.

concepts.name: Iteration Tax
concepts.description: The compounding time and resource cost of slow iteration cycles caused by discovering constraint violations late in development.

concepts.name: Continuous Deployment
concepts.description: Operational practices enabling dynamic, continuous updates to production ML models based on automated monitoring and feedback loops.

concepts.name: Spatial and Temporal Locality
concepts.description: An operating system assumption of predictable memory access patterns that stochastic ML data loading workloads fundamentally violate.

concepts.name: Data Drift
concepts.description: The degradation of model performance caused when production data distributions shift away from the original training dataset envelope.

concepts.name: Federated Learning
concepts.description: A distributed architecture that trains models across edge nodes without centralizing sensitive raw data to preserve privacy.

concepts.name: Tiered Storage
concepts.description: A data architecture that dynamically migrates datasets across hot, warm, and cold storage tiers based on access recency.

concepts.name: Store-and-forward Architecture
concepts.description: A network transmission strategy for intermittent connectivity that batches data locally and transmits it asynchronously during connectivity windows.

concepts.name: Data Lineage
concepts.description: The tracking of data provenance and metadata required for regulatory compliance and root cause analysis of model regressions.

concepts.name: Ablation Studies
concepts.description: A systematic experimental methodology that isolates individual component contributions to distinguish genuine architectural effects from statistical noise.

concepts.name: Data Cascade
concepts.description: The compounding technical debt and downstream system failures caused specifically by unmitigated data quality issues propagating forward.

concepts.name: Multi-Scale Feedback
concepts.description: The orchestration of monitoring loops across multiple timescales to enable both rapid operational correction and long-term strategic adaptation.
